message:
fix(compaction): use conservative 3-char/token estimate for code-heavy content

Two improvements to context window estimation accuracy:

1. CHARS_PER_TOKEN_ESTIMATE lowered from 4 to 3:
   The previous estimate of 4 chars/token was calibrated for English prose,
   but code and symbol-heavy content (JSON, tool calls, structured data)
   tokenizes more densely — often 2-3 chars per token. Using 3 provides a
   safer overestimate of token count, reducing the risk of exceeding context
   windows during compaction decisions.

2. json_byte_len error handling:
   Changed `let _ = serde_json::to_writer(...)` to an explicit if-let-Err
   pattern. While the Counter writer cannot fail, this makes the intent clear
   and handles theoretical edge cases (e.g., serde recursion limits) without
   silently ignoring the result.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
